title: Why does osmupdate return exit code 21 when there were no changes?
author: busti
body:
When there were no changes detected between the last run of osmupdate and the current one, osmupdate prints `Your OSM file is already up-to-date.` and exits with exit code 21. See:

https://github.com/kartoza/docker-osm/blob/develop/docker-osmupdate/osmupdate.c#L1527

This causes download.py to print the error message `An error occured in osmupdate. Let's try again.` and results in it not waiting the set time but instead just 2 seconds before retrying. See:

https://github.com/kartoza/docker-osm/blob/develop/docker-osmupdate/download.py#L153

This has resulted in some confusion in https://github.com/kartoza/docker-osm/issues/58

Is this exit code intentional? Or is it simply a typo?

title: Avoid using subprocess to call psql
author: frafra
body:
`psycopg2` is imported. It would be better to rely on it instead of requiring to install psql as additional dependency, in my opinion.

https://github.com/kartoza/docker-osm/blob/f3452495f9ddeb71f40662d3447e71ba4dcf5120/docker-imposm/importer.py#L271

title: enrich.py: string indices must be integers
author: frafra
body:
osmenrich fails:
```
dockerosm-osmenrich-1  | Error when processing 2022-09-20T16:58:07Z->-2022-09-20T17:00:09Z.osc.gz : string indices must be integers
```

Removing the wide and generic try/except here shows some details:

https://github.com/kartoza/docker-osm/blob/f3452495f9ddeb71f40662d3447e71ba4dcf5120/docker-osmenrich/enrich.py#L581-L582

```
dockerosm-osmenrich-1  | Traceback (most recent call last):
dockerosm-osmenrich-1  |   File "/home/enrich.py", line 618, in <module>
dockerosm-osmenrich-1  |     enrich.run()
dockerosm-osmenrich-1  |   File "/home/enrich.py", line 609, in run
dockerosm-osmenrich-1  |     self.enrich_database_from_diff_file()
dockerosm-osmenrich-1  |   File "/home/enrich.py", line 566, in enrich_database_from_diff_file
dockerosm-osmenrich-1  |     self.enrich_database_from_osm_data(
dockerosm-osmenrich-1  |   File "/home/enrich.py", line 508, in enrich_database_from_osm_data
dockerosm-osmenrich-1  |     osm_id = self.check_data_on_dict(
dockerosm-osmenrich-1  |   File "/home/enrich.py", line 301, in check_data_on_dict
dockerosm-osmenrich-1  |     return data[key]
dockerosm-osmenrich-1  | TypeError: string indices must be integers
dockerosm-osmenrich-1 exited with code 1
```

It looks that `data` can just be a string, such as `@id`.

title: imposm cannot be built
author: frafra
body: It looks like the base image is too old, and SSL certificates are not valid anymore.

title: Pin dependencies
author: frafra
body: Both #121 and #125 are errors generated from `requirements.txt` with non-pinned dependencies, thus producing unpredictable results.

This error appears periodically:

```
dockerosm-db-1         | 2022-09-20 18:06:51.338 UTC [1841] root@gis FATAL:  role "root" does not exist
```
This is due to a wrong health check.

title: No polygon layer in the PG container
author: Gustry
body:
I just tried the latest `develop` branch.

I don't have any polygon layer anymore : 
![Screenshot from 2021-09-30 12-41-54](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1609292/135440971-9ee85ec7-3645-4ab1-b8b1-4f4945ebeae1.png)

I don't think the issue in Docker-OSM, but we need to investigate why and where is the issue.

All polygons layer are recognised as invalid (tabular in the screenshot), like buildings, landscape

title: [RFC] Changes needed for integration tests
author: lucernae
body:
We need a way to test the repo changes as proposed [here](https://github.com/kartoza/docker-osm/pull/116#pullrequestreview-746149513):

I have no familiarity so far with the stack to determine if the images needs to have it's own unit test. So far we have 3 separate image under one repo. It may need a separate discussion.

At the moment, we can focus on an integration tests (tests that makes sure the stack works as one single services).

To do this, we need to have a clear goals on what needs to be tested:
- we should have small/minimum test dataset with predictable result, as the input
- what to test? connections? schema? final results of the data?
- if possible let’s use test framework like python unittest, so it can be described and added easily
- where to test? I guess we can only rely on GitHub action for now, since it uses docker-compose. hopefully it is under the github action test limit.

Requesting for comments from @timlinux @NyakudyaA @Gustry

title: Bitcoin miner in docker hub postgis 13 ?
author: jbjuin
body:
Hi,

it seems that there is a bitcoin miner in this image:

https://hub.docker.com/layers/kartoza/postgis/13/images/sha256-0ebcf54aa52c3c433307273c3f69a07952bb7f794f8d1ee91faaf611b974ae88?context=explore

We installed it few days ago on a machine and 2 days after the process `kdevtmpfsi` show up consuming 100% CPU.
See here for an issue describing the pb: https://github.com/docker-library/postgres/issues/798

I stoped the container, removed the image, rebooted and deployed the kartoza/posgis:11-2.5.
We already use this image elsewhere and it seems that the miner is not present.

Any idea why it appeared in this last image ?

title: Invalid url for geojson file in pbf-downloader
author: NyakudyaA

title: Exception in importer.py with mknod
author: bchartier
body:
Hi,
When I tested docker-osm I encountered an error with importer.py, more precisely with mknod function here:
https://github.com/kartoza/docker-osm/blob/develop/docker-imposm3/importer.py#L239
At runtime I got an exception telling me that mknod was not available.

I don't understand why this exception occured. I saw mknod was specific to Unix systems. I didn't investigate much on this. I fixed this by replacing mknod by pathlib.Path.touch.

I don't know if I missed some obvious configuration of the project. I can share my fix with a pull request if you like.

title: Rename PBF file
author: dobriseb
body:
Hi,

Thanks you for this work.

At first start, my DB was desperately empty.
I've seen that dockerosm_osmupdate was executing this command :
`bash -c while [ ! -f /home/settings/country.pbf ] ; do sleep 1; done && python3 -u /home/download.py`

So, I have to rename my .PBF file to country.pbf for it to start to import data.

Is that the normal way to do it ?
If yes, why isn't it documented in the Readme.md ?
